column5: What It Does
column6: Gap / Why Now
column10: Tech Stack
column14: Notes

column5: Turn MacBook into drum pad. Tap different zones for different sounds. Force-sensitive via accelerometer. Lid angle as pitch bend.
column6: SlapMac proved accelerometer apps go viral ($5K in 3 days). Open-source accel libraries exist. Only 2 apps in this space.
column10: Swift, IOKit HID, AVAudioEngine, menu bar
column14: Sell direct - no App Store 30% cut

column5: Watches ~/Downloads. AI reads filename + content (OCR for images/PDFs) and auto-moves to right folder. Learns your folder structure.
column6: Hazel is $42 with complex rules. Sparkle sends data to cloud. Floxtop is new/limited. No simple 'just works' AI organizer.
column10: Foundation Models, Vision (OCR), FSEvents, SwiftUI, menu bar
column14: macOS 26+ requirement. Zero ongoing cost.

column5: One-click meeting recorder. Captures system audio, transcribes in real-time, summarizes into action items. 100% on-device.
column6: Otter.ai ($16.99/mo) sends audio to cloud. No local-only meeting transcriber with summarization. Healthcare/legal need this.
column10: ScreenCaptureKit, Speech framework, Foundation Models, menu bar
column14: $0 infrastructure cost. Privacy is the killer feature.

column5: AI clipboard manager. Stores everything you copy. Semantic search by meaning, not exact text. Auto-categorizes: links, code, addresses, images.
column6: Paste is $3.99/mo subscription. Maccy has no AI. Cai is open-source/early. Clippr not launched yet. Clear 'missing middle'.
column10: NaturalLanguage (embeddings), Vision (OCR), NSPasteboard, SwiftUI, menu bar
column14: First mover with polish wins. Clipboard managers have high retention.

column5: Monitors App Store + Play Store reviews. AI categorizes (bug/feature/praise). Slack/email alerts. Reply from dashboard. Sentiment tracking.
column6: AppFollow $120+/mo, AppBot $49+/mo. Nothing under $10/mo for indie devs. Massive feature bloat in existing tools.
column10: App Store Connect API, Google Play API, LLM, Slack/Discord webhooks
column14: At $5/mo, 2000 customers = $120K ARR

column5: Native Mac bookmark + knowledge base. Save URLs/PDFs/notes. Auto-summarizes and categorizes on-device. Semantic search across everything.
column6: Every bookmark manager is a browser extension. Karakeep is self-hosted (friction). No native Mac app with semantic search.
column10: Foundation Models, NaturalLanguage (embeddings), SwiftUI, SwiftData
column14: High one-time price justified by depth of features.

column5: Drop files, AI reads content via OCR, suggests meaningful names. 'Receipt from Amazon - March 2026.pdf' instead of 'Document-3.pdf'.
column6: NameQuick ($49) requires API keys. No fully on-device intelligent batch renamer exists.
column10: Foundation Models, Vision (OCR), drag-and-drop UI, SwiftUI
column14: Universal pain point. Very simple to build.

column5: Connects to IMAP. Reads emails locally. Foundation Models summarizes into daily digest: action items, important threads, ignorable stuff.
column6: No Mac email tool does local-only AI summarization. Canary Mail is cloud-based. Privacy-focused users want this.
column10: Foundation Models, IMAP (MailCore2 or native), SwiftUI, menu bar
column14: Privacy angle is strong selling point.

column5: Tap to talk. Speech framework transcribes. Foundation Models structures into journal entry. Auto-detects mood, tracks patterns over time.
column6: Rosebud raised $6M but is cloud-based ($12.99/mo). No local voice-first journal. Mood tracking market = $1.5B growing to $5.7B.
column10: Speech framework, Foundation Models, HealthKit, WidgetKit, SwiftData
column14: Can correlate with Apple Health data for unique insights.

column5: Gamified menu bar for developers. Earn XP for commits, PRs, focus sessions. Character levels up. Streak tracking, daily quests.
column6: Zero gamified productivity apps for Mac menu bar. Nothing targets developers. Habitica/Forest are mobile-only.
column10: GitKit (local git monitoring), SwiftUI, menu bar, SpriteKit (pixel art)
column14: Natural virality in dev communities (Twitter/X screenshots).

column5: Connect GitHub repo. AI reads commits/PRs since last tag. Generates polished App Store / Play Store 'What's New' text.
column6: No tool generates consumer-facing app store release notes from git history. All changelog tools target web SaaS.
column10: GitHub API, LLM, web dashboard
column14: 500K+ iOS/Android developers as TAM.

column5: Simple review monitor for indie devs. Reviews in Slack/Discord. AI categorizes. Reply from dashboard. Weekly sentiment summary.
column6: Same gap as ReviewPilot but even simpler. $5/mo vs competitors at $50+/mo. Instatus proved simple tools hit $40K MRR.
column10: App Store Connect API, Google Play API, Slack webhooks, LLM
column14: Could merge with ReviewPilot as single product.

column5: Visual .env file editor. Share configs without committing secrets. Team sync via encrypted link.
column6: Doppler is $20/user/mo (enterprise). 90% of indie devs use raw .env files. SheetBest does $18K MRR with similar simplicity.
column10: Swift CLI, encryption, SwiftUI Mac app, CloudKit or custom sync
column14: Natural developer virality. CLI-first approach.

column5: TestFlight + waitlist manager. Manage beta programs, invite codes, graduated rollout, structured feedback collection from testers.
column6: No waitlist tool targets app launches. GetWaitlist ($15/mo) is generic. Nothing integrates with TestFlight.
column10: App Store Connect API, TestFlight API, web dashboard, iOS SDK
column14: Niche but high-value customers (app developers).

column5: AI worksheet & quiz generator. 'Generate 10 algebra problems, 8th grade'. Auto-generates printable PDF with answer key.
column6: Teachers manually create worksheets. No simple AI tool for this. 1M+ private tutors in US alone.
column10: LLM, PDF generation, web dashboard
column14: Could expand to full tutor management platform.

column5: Ambient soundscapes that react to typing rhythm. Rain intensity follows typing speed. Pausing fades to silence. Fast coding builds crescendo.
column6: Haptyk does keyboard sounds. Nobody does reactive ambient soundscapes tied to work rhythm.
column10: Accelerometer, keystroke monitoring, AVAudioEngine, menu bar
column14: Great complement to MacTap using same tech.

column5: Pixel pet in menu bar powered by Foundation Models. Has personality, opinions about your work habits. Feed it by completing focus sessions.
column6: Mac Pet exists but is just a Pomodoro timer. No desktop pet uses on-device AI for actual personality.
column10: Foundation Models, SpriteKit, Screen Time API, SwiftUI, menu bar
column14: High shareability. Pet skins as upsell.

column5: Upload PDF contract. AI highlights risky clauses in plain English. 'This clause means they own everything you create.'
column6: Freelancers sign bad contracts. Lawyer review = $200+. No affordable tool gives plain-English risk summary.
column10: Foundation Models or LLM API, PDFKit, Vision (OCR), SwiftUI
column14: Can be local Mac app (Foundation Models) or web SaaS.

column5: Snap photo of paper menu. OCR extracts items. Generates hosted digital menu with QR code. Restaurant updates via simple dashboard.
column6: Small restaurants use paper menus or bad PDFs. No photo-to-digital-menu tool. 1M+ small restaurants in US.
column10: Vision/OCR API, web hosting, QR generation, simple CMS
column14: B2B recurring revenue. High retention once adopted.

column5: Point at any line of code. AI reads the commit + PR + surrounding context. Explains WHY the code was changed in plain English.
column6: git blame tells WHO not WHY. No tool explains reasoning behind code changes.
column10: Git APIs, LLM, VS Code Extension API
column14: Could also be a Mac app or CLI tool.

column5: Camera scans fridge/pantry. Vision identifies ingredients. Foundation Models generates recipes from what you have. Tracks expiration.
column6: Existing recipe apps need manual input. No camera-based + on-device AI recipe generation. Food waste = $400B problem.
column10: Vision, Foundation Models, SwiftUI, SwiftData, WidgetKit, CloudKit
column14: Foundation Models = zero API cost for recipe generation.

column5: Aggregates all Apple Health data into widget-first dashboard. Foundation Models generates daily health briefing in plain English.
column6: Apple Health is data-rich but insight-poor. Window before Apple ships Health+ (rumored late 2026).
column10: HealthKit, Foundation Models, WidgetKit, WatchKit, Charts, SwiftUI
column14: Time-sensitive: beat Apple Health+ to market.

column5: Learns phone usage patterns. Finds optimal 2-minute windows for micro-habits. Live Activity nudges at perfect moments.
column6: Habit apps remind at fixed times. None analyze behavior to find when you're most receptive. Called out in 'ideas that don't exist' lists.
column10: Foundation Models, DeviceActivity, ActivityKit, WidgetKit, SwiftUI
column14: Novel concept with zero competition.

column5: Detects sedentary periods via Watch sensors. Guides 60-second desk stretches with haptic coaching. Tracks mobility score over time.
column6: Apple's Stand reminders are binary. No watch-first guided movement app. Remote work = huge TAM.
column10: WatchKit, HealthKit, CoreMotion, SwiftUI, haptic feedback
column14: Watch-first differentiator in growing market.

column5: Snap receipt photo. Vision extracts merchant/date/items/total. Auto-categorizes expenses. Tracks warranty periods. Live Activity alerts.
column6: Receipt apps are clunky or business-focused. No simple consumer app combines OCR + warranty tracking + Live Activities.
column10: Vision (OCR), Foundation Models, ActivityKit, SwiftData, WidgetKit
column14: Perennial need. Tax season drives downloads.

column5: Pomodoro timer in Dynamic Island. Layerable ambient sounds. Integrates with Apple Music. Logs focus as HealthKit mindful minutes.
column6: $3.99 one-time
column9: Few Pomodoro apps use Dynamic Island well. None combine ambient audio + Dynamic Island + HealthKit.
column10: ActivityKit, AVFoundation, HealthKit, MusicKit, WidgetKit, SwiftUI
column14: Quickest iOS app to ship. Proven demand.

column5: Camera identifies plants on-device. Generates care schedule based on your location's climate via WeatherKit. Watering reminders via widgets.
column6: PictureThis charges $30+/yr and uses cloud. On-device = faster, private, cheaper. Plant parents are passionate niche.
column10: Vision, CreateML, Foundation Models, CoreLocation, WeatherKit, WidgetKit
column14: Model training is longest part. Could use existing open models.

column5: Scan book ISBN. Track reading sessions. After finishing Foundation Models generates discussion questions and theme analysis. Reading streak widget.
column6: Goodreads is universally hated. No reading tracker uses on-device AI for book analysis. BookTok community is massive.
column10: Vision (barcode), Foundation Models, WidgetKit, SwiftData, CloudKit
column14: BookTok/reading community is highly engaged and vocal.

column5: Smart power nap timer. Detects actual sleep onset via Watch biometrics. Wakes at optimal sleep cycle point. Logs to HealthKit.
column6: $3.99 one-time
column9: No nap-specific app uses real Watch biometric data for optimal wake timing. All timers are dumb countdowns.
column10: WatchKit, HealthKit, CoreMotion, WKExtendedRuntimeSession, SwiftUI
column14: Simple concept with real value. Watch-first.

column5: Scan restaurant receipt. Diners join via SharePlay. Each taps their items. Calculates individual totals with tax/tip. Live Activity for all.
column6: Free + $1.99 remove ads
column9: Splitwise is for IOUs. No app combines receipt OCR + SharePlay for real-time collaborative splitting. Constant Reddit request.
column10: Vision (OCR), SharePlay, ActivityKit, MultipeerConnectivity, SwiftUI
column14: Viral potential via SharePlay group sessions.

column5: Point camera at electronics. Vision identifies model. Gives instant fair-market resale price. AR overlay shows price floating above device.
column6: Free / $1.99/mo
column9: No point-and-scan pricing app for used electronics. Perfect for garage sales and Marketplace meetups.
column10: Vision, ARKit, Foundation Models, Speech framework, SwiftUI
column14: Needs price data API. Could scrape eBay/Swappa.

column5: Visual Siri Shortcuts builder + curated library. Describe what you want in English and AI builds the shortcut. Community marketplace.
column6: Free / $4.99/mo
column9: Apple's Shortcuts app is powerful but intimidating. No good Shortcuts marketplace. iOS 26 added AI shortcuts.
column10: Foundation Models, SiriKit, App Intents, SwiftUI, CloudKit, StoreKit 2
column14: Longer build but high ceiling. iOS 26 momentum.

column5: Create walking/running routes with AR waypoints. Others follow with AR arrows overlaid on camera. Community-created walking directions.
column6: Free / $2.99/mo to create routes
column9: Google/Apple Maps optimize for cars. No AR walking route creator. Tourism + fitness communities would love this.
column10: ARKit, RealityKit, MapKit, CoreLocation, CloudKit, HealthKit, ActivityKit
column14: Longer build. Strong community moat once established.

column5: AI-powered QA agent for iOS apps. Describe tests in plain English. Claude autonomously navigates app in Simulator, verifies functionality, generates pass/fail reports. Learns from past runs.
column6: No autonomous AI QA tool for iOS exists. Competitors: Appium (manual scripts), XCUITest (code-heavy), Maestro (YAML scripts), QA Wolf ($4K+/mo web-only), TestRigor (~$500/mo). NoobQA = zero scripting, plain English, sees inside app via NooberMCP.
column10: Swift, Claude API (claude run), NooberMCP, Figma MCP, Linear MCP, Slack MCP, SwiftUI, GitHub Actions
column14: ALREADY BUILT. Core product feature-complete. Has: dual-mode testing (Simulator + Dev), learned lessons, CI/CD template, integrations (Linear/Slack/Figma), onboarding wizard, CLI tool, git hooks. Website on Vercel. Revenue potential: 200 indie + 50 team + 10 pro = ~$15K MRR.

column5: In-app debugging SDK for iOS. Network inspector, log viewer, environment switcher, mock rules, flow recorder. Companion Mac app for remote debugging.
column6: Existing tools: Flipper (Meta, deprecated vibes), FLEX (open-source but unmaintained), Proxyman ($69-$89 for network only). No all-in-one debugging SDK with companion app + MCP bridge for AI agents.
column10: Swift, SwiftUI, Bonjour/Network.framework, URLProtocol, MCP server
column14: ALREADY BUILT. Has: network inspection, log viewer, environment switching, mock/intercept rules, flow recorder, deep link tester, companion Mac app, MCP server for AI agents. Unique moat: MCP bridge enables AI testing (NoobQA depends on it).
